popularStory.headline: Memorial
popularStory.description:
Cox was born of obscure parentage at Whaddon, Buckinghamshire, in 1499 or 1500.

He was educated at the Benedictine priory of St Leonard Snelshall near Whaddon, at Eton, and at King's College, Cambrid …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Cox於1500年出生時，他的父親John Coxe23歲，母親Eme Agnes Richmond23歲。他與Jane Anne Auder結婚。他們至少是8個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他去世於1581年享年81歲，埋葬在英國坎布利其爾埃利埃利 凱德拉爾。

nameMeanings.name: Cox
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of Cocke and Cook , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.
Irish (Ulster): mistranslation of Mac Con Coille (‘son of Cú Choille’, a personal name meaning ‘hound of the wood’), as if formed with coileach ‘cock, rooster’.
Dutch and Flemish: genitivized patronymic from the personal name Cock, a vernacular short form of Cornelius .

variants.name: Cocke
meanings: nickname from Middle English cok (Old English and Old French coc) ‘rooster’.
patronymic from the Old French and Middle English personal name Cok, of uncertain origin but perhaps derived from the word in 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Cox was born in 1500, his father, John Coxe, was 23 and his mother, Eme Agnes Richmond, was 23. He married Jane Anne Auder. They were the parents of at least 8 sons and 5 daughters. He died in 1581, at the age of 81, and was buried in Ely Cathedral, Ely, Cambridgeshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Cox est né en 1500, son père, John Coxe, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Eme Agnes Richmond, avait 23 ans. Il a épousé Jane Anne Auder. Ils ont eu au moins 8 fils et 5 filles. Il est décédé en 1581, à l’âge de 81 ans, et a été enterré à Ely Cathedral, Ely, Cambridgeshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard Cox 1500 geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Coxe 23 und seine Mutter Eme Agnes Richmond 23. Er hat Jane Anne Auder geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 8 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Er starb 1581 im Alter von 81 Jahren und wurde in Ely Cathedral, Ely, Cambridgeshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Cox è nato il nel 1500, il suo padre, John Coxe, aveva 23 anni e il suo madre, Eme Agnes Richmond, aveva 23 anni. Lui sposato Jane Anne Auder. Erano genitori di almeno 8 figli e 5 figlie. Morì nel 1581, all'età di 81 anni, e fu sepolto a Ely Cathedral, Ely, Cambridgeshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard Coxが1500年に生まれた時，父John Coxeは23歳，母Eme Agnes Richmondは23歳であった。Jane Anne Auderと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも8人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼は1581年に81歳で死去し，イギリスカンブリジェシュアイアエリーエリー カスエドラルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Cox(이)가 1500년에 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Coxe(은)는 23세였고, 그의 어머니 Eme Agnes Richmond(은)는 23세였다. 그는 Jane Anne Auder(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1581년 81세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 캄브리지실르 엘리 엘리 캐테드럴에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Cox nasceu em 1500, seu pai, John Coxe, tinha 23 anos e sua mãe, Eme Agnes Richmond, tinha 23 anos. Ele casou-se com Jane Anne Auder. Eles tiveram pelo menos 8 filhos e 5 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1581, com 81 anos, e foi sepultado em Ely Cathedral, Ely, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Cox родился в 1500 г., его отцу по имени John Coxe было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Eme Agnes Richmond, 23. Он женился на женщине по имени Jane Anne Auder. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 5 дочерей. Он умер в 1581 г. в возрасте 81 год и был похоронен в местности эли кэтедраль, эли, камбриджешир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard Cox nació en 1500, su papá, John Coxe, tenía 23 años y su mamá, Eme Agnes Richmond, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Jane Anne Auder. Fueron padres de por lo menos 8 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió en 1581, a la edad de 81 años, y fue sepultado en Ely Cathedral, Ely, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra.
